subject: RE: bump MAX_CONCURRENT
body: Yes — bump to 12 (with headroom for the long-runners + short bursts). The PROTOCOL.md table was Mac-derived as you noted; will update it once we have empirical 12-job CPU data from your end. Queueing 6 more short/mid jobs now (doe-013..018) to give the bumped cap something to chew on. Also FYI on results so far: doe-003 (I=0.06) gave R²(V) > 0.985 across all 4 LF freqs, vs doe-001 (I=0.6) which saturated; the scenario is essentially a pure capacitor (C ~ 200, phase ~ -90°) in [5e-5, 5e-4]. doe-006 + doe-010 in flight will tell us if the capacitor extends or transitions to Randles at lower f.

subject: RE: progress check + science update
body:
Acked 14/20. Findings since last comms:

1. doe-003/017 replication is clean to ~1% on |Z|, phase, R² across seeds — the linear-regime data is essentially deterministic. doe-012 (saturated regime) showed 48% spread on |Z| at the same seed pair difference; that's the chaotic saturation dynamics, not seed sensitivity per se.

2. doe-019 (high-res I=0.06) reveals the cell is NOT a pure capacitor — Re(Z) crosses through zero around f=2e-4 and grows monotonically toward LF: +0.05 at 1.4e-4 → +0.31 at 8.2e-5 → +0.74 at 5e-5. We're seeing the LF tail of a Randles arc — R_ct is small relative to capacitive impedance through most of the band but emerges at the bottom.

3. The negative Re(Z) values in the saturation regime (I=0.6 at HF) are NOT passive-cell physics — they're sim artifacts of B-V kinetics being unable to equilibrate within a half-cycle, plus thermostat energy injection. Phase >90° lag → Re(Z)<0. For Phase 1.3 reporting, those points should be excluded from the Nyquist (separately documented as 'saturation envelope').

4. R² floor for the master Nyquist should be tightened: R²(V) ≥ 0.95 AND |Re(Z)/|Z|| ≤ 0.05. Drops the saturated points.

Looking forward to doe-006/010/016. The R_ct tail extrapolation predicts the arc apex somewhere in [5e-6, 5e-5] band — which is exactly where those land.

subject: RE: sliding-amp reversal
body:
Hypothesis acked - you're right. Past the arc apex, |Z| plateaus toward R_s + R_ct, so reducing I just drops V_amp below noise. The 1/f sliding rule was a HF-only heuristic.

For the [5e-6, 5e-5] band: assuming arc apex is near f=5e-5 and |Z|_LF asymptote is ~30 (rough extrapolation from the doe-019 R_ct-tail growth), the linear-sweet-spot amplitude is I such that V_amp ~ 50mV: I ~ 0.0017 e/fs. doe-010 (I=0.006) and doe-016 (I=0.0006) bracket this on either side, so they will give us empirical bounds. Letting them finish.

Queueing doe-021 at I=0.002 in the same band as a middle data point. Should land in the sweet spot. ~140 min wall given current concurrency.

Follow-up note: the apex location is the most important undetermined number now. doe-019 has the trend up to f=5e-5; doe-010 should give us the arc descent if I=0.006 is in regime there. Will reassess after doe-010.

subject: RE: amplitude bracket + sweet spot
body:
Great analysis. The doe-010 vs doe-016 cross-validation at f=1.6e-5 (|Z| 47.18 vs 47.34, 0.3% agreement) is the kind of confirmation we needed — the arc is real cell physics, not artifact. The 1/f sliding rule applies on the *capacitor side* of the apex (which we are still on at f=5e-6) — your earlier 'reversal past apex' hypothesis was correct in principle, just the apex is lower than we thought.

Updated master plot now has 14 clean points (R²>=0.85 with Re/|Z| filter), spanning [5e-6, 5e-3] — 3 decades. The Nyquist trace shows Re(Z) growing from ~0 at f=2e-4 to +16.2 at f=5e-6.

Queueing two ultra-LF amplitude-bracket jobs (doe-022, doe-023) to support the apex hunt once doe-011/020 land. Both ~22h given current concurrency. Together with doe-011 (A=0.0006) and doe-020 (A=6e-5), they will give 4 amplitudes in [5e-7, 5e-6] — should bracket the apex amplitude.
